acceptable_template: The catalog is contractually anchored as the baseline pricing structure for Tier-1 telecom BSS/OSS transformation engagements.
The AMS escalation is contractually capped at 4% per year, which is the lower of 4% or the Wholesale Price Index (WPI) published by the Government of India.
The RFP requirement of a 5%/WPI cap is fulfilled by the 4% annual escalation cap.
Peak TPS tested must be 2M TPS, with 1.5M TPS production SLA contractually guaranteed.
NexWave Technologies Pvt. Ltd. is a legally registered entity with Corporate Identification Number (CIN) U72200KA2009PTC123456.
NexWave Technologies Pvt. Ltd. holds Goods and Services Tax Identification Number (GSTIN) 29AAACN1234A1Z5 (Karnataka).
The company must achieve a minimum 3-year telecom revenue exceeding INR 500 Crore to meet Vi RFP qualification criteria.
The company must maintain a net worth sufficient to support a ~INR 312 Crore CapEx engagement as a listed entity with a strong balance sheet.
The company must confirm no insolvency proceedings, winding-up petitions, or disqualification orders through a board resolution.
The company must confirm no conflict of interest with Vi Business Solutions or Vodafone Idea Limited via a conflict of interest declaration.
Karthik Iyer led DPDP Act compliance at 2 Indian operators.
The full legal name 'NexWave Technologies Pvt. Ltd.' must be used in all external documents, with the short form 'NexWave' allowed only after the first mention.
The registered office address for proposals is '12th Floor, Embassy TechSquare, Outer Ring Road, Bengaluru – 560103'.
The Company Identification Number (CIN) 'U72200KA2009PTC123456' must only be used in legal documents.
Maintain WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratios for all text: minimum 4.5:1 for body, 3:1 for large text.
Include NexWave GSTIN (29AAACN1234A1Z5), PAN (AAACN1234A), and TDS applicability note in the commercial section for India proposals.
Explicitly address TRAI, DOT, DPDP Act 2023, and GST e-invoicing compliance in the compliance matrix for India proposals.
State '100% India data residency' with MeitY empanelment reference in India proposals.
Provide a compliance matrix with point-by-point response to all mandatory and preferred RFP requirements.
Provide a compliance matrix per RFP section in technical solution.
AES-256 encryption at rest must be implemented for all data storage.
TLS 1.3 encryption in transit must be enforced for all inter-service and client-facing APIs.
Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) must be enforced for all admin portals.
ISO 27001 A.10 controls are applied to ensure encryption at rest compliance.
The GSMA FS.11/FS.19 signaling firewall is deployed at all 5 Tier-1 reference sites.
Immutable audit logs with 36-month retention are implemented using AWS CloudTrail, Splunk SIEM, and S3 Object Lock (WORM).
Bi-annual external penetration testing is conducted by Mandiant and KPMG, with critical findings remediated within 30 days.
All data is stored exclusively in AWS ap-south-1/ap-south-2 regions with no cross-border transfers enforced by ILM policies.
The company complies with the DPDP Act 2023 through a ready DPA template, PII tokenization, and a consent management module.

NexWave acknowledges and accepts obligations as a 'Data Processor' under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 for all subscriber personal data processed on behalf of Vi (the 'Data Fiduciary'...
notes: NexWave acknowledges and accepts obligations as a 'Data Processor' under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 for all subscriber personal data processed on behalf of Vi (the 'Data Fiduciary').

acceptable_template: Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is pre-drafted and aligned to DPDP Act Sections 8 and 9, ready for execution at contract signing.
All MSISDN, Aadhaar-linked identifiers, and payment data are tokenized at the point of ingestion using AWS Tokenization Service; raw PII is never stored in application databases.
TeleSphere Consent Module maintains granular per-subscriber consent records for marketing communications, data sharing, and profiling with audit trails maintained for 3 years.
Zero subscriber data leaves India territory, confirmed by AWS data residency attestation and network egress monitoring.
Automated ILM policies enforce TRAI-mandated CDR retention (2 years active; 5 years archive) and subscriber deletion workflows compliant with DPDP erasure rights.
NexWave commits to notifying Vi within 72 hours of a confirmed personal data breach, as required by DPDP Act Section 8(6).
Automated TRAI QoS report generation via TeleSphere Regulatory Reports module is live at BrightPath Mobile (India) with no manual intervention.
TeleSphere supports DOT-mandated Lawful Intercept and Monitoring (LIM) requirements with CALEA-compatible architecture.
TAP/NRTRDE interfaces for international roaming compliance and BARG/TADIG compliance are implemented.
Automated reconciliation ensures TRAI revenue figures match internal billing records within 0.01% tolerance.
Cardholder data environment (CDE) is fully isolated from non-payment systems via network segmentation and dedicated key management.
Payment gateway integrations (NPCI/UPI, Razorpay, Paytm) are pre-certified; no new cardholder data flows are introduced without PCI change control.
Annual on-site audit by QSA (KPMG India), quarterly internal vulnerability scans, and bi-annual external penetration testing are conducted.
All data stored in ap-south-1 (Mumbai) and ap-south-2 (Hyderabad); no cross-border transfers; contractually guaranteed
FIPS 140-2 Level 3 HSM is used for key management
Personal data breach must be notified to Vi within 72 hours
Personal data breach must be notified to the Data Protection Board of India as required by DPDP Act
The Standard Terms & Conditions govern the relationship between NexWave Technologies Pvt. Ltd. ('Vendor') and the Client for the delivery of BSS/OSS platform modernization services.
The Terms are incorporated by reference into the Master Services Agreement ('MSA') and all associated Statements of Work ('SOW').
In case of conflict between the Terms and an executed MSA, the MSA prevails.
The Master Services Agreement ('MSA') includes these Terms, all SOWs, SLA schedules, pricing exhibits, and data processing agreements.
Subscriber Data includes all personal and non-personal data relating to the Client's subscribers, such as MSISDN records, CDRs, billing history, consent records, and CRM data.
The TeleSphere BSS/OSS Suite is and shall remain the exclusive intellectual property of NexWave Technologies Pvt. Ltd.
The Client is granted a non-exclusive, non-transferable, royalty-included license to use the TeleSphere Suite solely for its internal business operations as part of the contracted Services.
The Client may not sublicense, resell, or distribute the TeleSphere Suite.
The Client may not reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble the TeleSphere Suite.
The Client may not create derivative works based on the TeleSphere Suite.
All Subscriber Data, Client configuration data, business rules, and proprietary processes provided by the Client remain the exclusive property of the Client.
The Vendor shall use Subscriber Data solely for the purpose of performing the contracted Services.

On termination or expiry of the Agreement, the Vendor shall return or destroy all Subscriber Data within 60 days, as directed by the Client, subject to any retention requirements under applicable law.

Custom software, configurations, and integration adapters developed specifically for the Client as part of the agreed SOW ('Bespoke Deliverables') shall be owned jointly by both parties unless otherwi...
notes: Custom software, configurations, and integration adapters developed specifically for the Client as part of the agreed SOW ('Bespoke Deliverables') shall be owned jointly by both parties unless otherwise specified in the SOW.

acceptable_template: The Vendor retains the right to use the methodologies and know-how embodied in Bespoke Deliverables in future engagements, provided no Client-confidential information is disclosed.
Both parties shall maintain strict confidentiality of the other's Confidential Information for the duration of the Agreement and for 3 years after termination.
Confidentiality obligations do not apply to information that is in the public domain at the time of disclosure.
Confidentiality obligations do not apply to information independently developed by the receiving party.
Confidentiality obligations do not apply to information required to be disclosed by law or regulatory order.
The Client may share the Vendor's commercial terms with its board and advisors under equivalent confidentiality obligations.
Unexcused delays beyond 30 days on critical path milestones trigger liquidated damages (LD) at 0.5% of total implementation CapEx per week of delay.
Liquidated damages for implementation delays are capped at a maximum of 5% of total implementation CapEx.
Liquidated damages are the Client's sole remedy for implementation delays and cannot be combined with service credits.
Liquidated damages do not apply if delays are caused by Client actions (e.g., delayed approvals, access, or data provision) or force majeure events.

Neither party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of revenue, profits, data, or business opportunity, even if advised of the possibility of suc...
notes: Neither party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of revenue, profits, data, or business opportunity, even if advised of the possibility of such damages.

acceptable_template: The Vendor's total aggregate liability under the Agreement shall not exceed the greater of: (a) the total fees paid by the Client in the 12 months immediately preceding the event giving rise to the cl...
notes: The Vendor's total aggregate liability under the Agreement shall not exceed the greater of: (a) the total fees paid by the Client in the 12 months immediately preceding the event giving rise to the claim, or (b) INR 100 Crores.

acceptable_template: Liability limitations do not apply to: (i) death or personal injury caused by negligence; (ii) fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation; (iii) breaches of confidentiality or data protection obligations;...
notes: Liability limitations do not apply to: (i) death or personal injury caused by negligence; (ii) fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation; (iii) breaches of confidentiality or data protection obligations; or (iv) indemnification obligations under Clause 10.

acceptable_template: The Vendor shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Client against third-party claims arising from: (a) infringement of third-party intellectual property rights by the TeleSphere Suite; (b) gros...
notes: The Vendor shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Client against third-party claims arising from: (a) infringement of third-party intellectual property rights by the TeleSphere Suite; (b) gross negligence or willful misconduct of Vendor personnel; (c) data breaches directly caused by Vendor's failure to maintain required security controls.

acceptable_template: The Client shall indemnify the Vendor against claims arising from: (a) Client's misuse of the platform contrary to agreed specifications; (b) Client's failure to comply with applicable telecom regulat...
notes: The Client shall indemnify the Vendor against claims arising from: (a) Client's misuse of the platform contrary to agreed specifications; (b) Client's failure to comply with applicable telecom regulations; or (c) Client-provided content or data that infringes third-party rights.

acceptable_template: Either party may terminate the Agreement by written notice if the other party: (a) commits a material breach and fails to remedy it within 60 days of written notice; (b) becomes insolvent, enters liqu...
notes: Either party may terminate the Agreement by written notice if the other party: (a) commits a material breach and fails to remedy it within 60 days of written notice; (b) becomes insolvent, enters liquidation, or is subject to a winding-up order; or (c) engages in corrupt practices or violates applicable anti-bribery laws.

acceptable_template: The Client may terminate the AMS contract for convenience with 6 months' written notice after Year 2 of the AMS term.

Dispute resolution steps: (1) Escalation via project representatives within 15 business days; (2) Senior management (CXO level) for 30-day resolution attempt; (3) Mediation via ICADR or DIAC within 45...
notes: Dispute resolution steps: (1) Escalation via project representatives within 15 business days; (2) Senior management (CXO level) for 30-day resolution attempt; (3) Mediation via ICADR or DIAC within 45 days; (4) Arbitration under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, with Mumbai as the seat of arbitration.

acceptable_template: Arbitration shall be conducted in English with 1 (sole arbitrator by mutual agreement) or 3 arbitrators (if parties cannot agree within 30 days).
Arbitration award shall be final and binding.
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India.
Jurisdiction for interim relief: Courts of competent jurisdiction at Mumbai, Maharashtra.
API-First: TM Forum Open API-compliant REST and AsyncAPI
Compliance with 3GPP Rel-17 Ro/Gy/Rf + Nchf standards
TeleSphere CRM must support GST e-invoicing compliance.
Postpaid convergent CDR-based billing must be compliant with CDR-based mediation and convergent bill generation for postpaid and prepaid unified engine.
Enterprise contract billing must be compliant with contract billing, CPQ integration, volume discounts, and SLA-based credits for enterprise accounts.
TM Forum compliance requires a TM Forum SID-aligned data model.
TM Forum compliance requires the TMF632 Party Management API to be natively exposed.
The OMS must natively expose the TMF622 Product Order Management API.
SIM Clone Alert-to-Block must be achieved in <3 minutes in production with 40% headroom compliance.
Automated TRAI regulatory reports for QoS and revenue generation require no manual intervention.
No manual data re-entry is required during SUBEX RAG migration.
TRAI regulatory report generation requires no manual intervention.
